reviewer: agent matrix pass (headless Chrome, real download+run, light+dark, mobile+desktop) — revision 2

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — REAL WASM run: fresh profile → Download (~34 MB) → auto-init → ready, then Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-s embedded the 6 sample passages and a prefixed query entirely in a Web Worker; 0 console errors. conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — loads-clean=pass, calls-real-inference=pass. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/mobile.png

Agent verdict — real CLS-pooled 384-d vectors returned (rDim read back = 384). Prefixed query 'How do I recover access to my locked account?' ranked the correct passage 'To reset your password, open Settings and choose Forgot password.' at #1 (cosine 0.653); unrelated science passages scored lower. Output shape + semantics correct, not fake. conformance: 0✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — io-shape-semantic=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/mobile.png

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 5✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — declares-model-id=pass (Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-s linked), declares-task=pass (feature-extraction), declares-backend=pass (WASM), declares-quantisation=pass (q8), inference-off-main-thread=pass (new Worker). screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop-dark.png

Agent verdict — fresh profile shows 'Download model (~34 MB)'; after load the shared createModelLoader auto-inits to 'Model ready' with a 'Clear cached model' control + storage detail. HF ONNX fetched THROUGH Cache Storage (Snowflake/snowflake-arctic-embed-s URL) so a returning visit is scannable → auto-init, no re-download. conformance: 1✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — shared-auto-init-loader=pass, auto-init-on-cached=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png

Agent verdict — download progress streamed on the loader; a Retry button surfaces on failure (model-loader.js); errors route to .status.err regions on every control; ONNX bytes cached for offline reuse. Runs on the WASM path (no GPU); the headless no-GPU run produced real vectors, so it just works without WebGPU. conformance: 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 2◍ — live-status-region=pass, errors-on-the-page=pass, honest-progress-retry-offline=manual, runs-without-webgpu-fallback=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/mobile.png

Agent verdict — Embed & compare, Reset examples, prefixed-query search input + Search, inspect-vector select, with/without-prefix A/B button, and the three-model compare button are all real, labelled, and keyboard-operable (Enter submits the query). No dead controls. conformance: 0✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — controls-operable=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/mobile.png

Agent verdict — the matrix, ranking, vector strip and A/B numbers are all computed from the on-device worker's real embeddings (verified: 384-d, correct #1 ranking, A/B shift measured live as +0.647/0.876/-0.229). No canned result anywhere. conformance: 0✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — no-fake-output=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png

conformance (wasm, headless no-GPU): 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 0◍ — images-have-alt=pass, buttons-have-names=pass. The similarity matrix uses a visually-hidden <caption> + th scope=col/row; the projection SVG carries role=img + aria-label; status regions are role=status aria-live. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png

Agent verdict — desktop 1280×800 (light+dark) and mobile 360×740 DPR3 reviewed: no horizontal overflow (measured documentElement.scrollWidth−innerWidth = 0px at mobile), legible text, the at-a-glance table + cosine matrix sit in overflow-x:auto scroll containers, grid-2 collapses to one column on mobile. conformance: 2✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass, parity-both-classes=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop-dark.png, reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/mobile.png
rubric.notes: Agent-verified: 0px mobile overflow, light+dark both clean.

rubric.evidence: Agent verdict — all embedding runs in a Web Worker; the PCA power-iteration runs only over a handful of already-computed 384-d vectors on the main thread (cheap, n≪d); main-thread stayed responsive during load + embed. conformance: 0✓ 0✗ 0▨ 1◍ — inp-stays-low=manual. screenshots: reports/responsive/screens/arctic-embed/desktop.png

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up long main-thread tasks / yield so inference doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all embedding runs off the main thread in a Web Worker; only the small PCA over n≪d vectors runs on the main thread.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Keep heavy JS off the interaction path to keep INP low.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — clicking Embed/Search posts to the worker and awaits a message; no blocking loop on the interaction path.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: headless: main-thread responsive during embed; 0 console errors

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Semantic tables (caption + th scope), label every control, announce state in a live region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — sim-matrix has a visually-hidden caption + th scope=col/row; projection SVG has role=img+aria-label; role=status aria-live loader; labelled buttons/inputs/select.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Fluid layout; put wide content (tables) in overflow-x:auto so the page body never scrolls horizontally.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — matrix-scroll + cmp-table wrappers use overflow-x:auto; grid-2 uses auto-fit minmax so it collapses on mobile.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.

openQuestions: Would arctic-embed-m (768-d) be worth adding as a separate demo for a quality/size trade-off? (Additive, not a replacement.)
Should the A/B include a small multi-passage corpus to show the prefix's corpus-scale ranking benefit more convincingly than a single pair?

followUpGoals.goal: Add a WebGPU execution-provider fast path for arctic-embed (honest WASM fallback retained) as an additive enhancement.
Consider a small labelled retrieval set on the A/B panel so the prefix's nDCG-style benefit is visible over a corpus, not just a pair.

summary: Revision-2 agent matrix pass for arctic-embed: real download + on-device run verified in headless Chrome (prefixed query ranked the correct passage #1 at cosine 0.653; 384-d CLS vectors; live A/B prefix shift), light+dark and mobile+desktop screenshots reviewed (0px overflow, legible, operable controls), console clean. The A/B panel is framed HONESTLY: on a single pair the prefix often lowers the absolute cosine, and the copy says so — its benefit is corpus-scale ranking (MTEB), not one number. Auto-checkable conformance assertions pass (14/14 tested); manual-evidenced dimensions carry the agent verdict above.
